author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Fresh `jj git clone` (and `git clone`) writes only
`+refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*` to `.git/config`, so the
jjforge `refs/jjf/*` namespace is unreachable via plain
`git fetch`. `jjf pull` worked around this with an explicit
refspec on its inner `git fetch`, but users running raw git or
just `jjf ls` after a clone saw an empty planner.

## Recommendation

Implemented in the F-001/002/003 bundle. `jjf init` and `jjf
remote add` now write `+refs/jjf/*:refs/remotes/<remote>/jjf/*`
to `.git/config` (additive, idempotent); `jjf pull` writes it
lazily on first run as a safety net. After init on a fresh
clone, plain `git fetch <remote>` round-trips the namespace.

## Confidence

HIGH. Verified in the integration test
`init_writes_jjf_fetch_refspec_for_existing_remotes` and via
manual repro at
`scratchpad/qa/fix-verify/`: pre-init bob has no
`refs/jjf/*`; post-init `git config --get-all
remote.origin.fetch` shows both refspecs; `git fetch origin`
brings alice's issue ref into `refs/remotes/origin/jjf/issues/`.

## Open follow-ups

None.
